summary: A React and TypeScript fan tool for Project Moon focused on literary origins, canto context, and cross-game lore connections. The shared data layer and lore graph MVP are implemented. The current milestone is M3 content completion and consistency.

milestones.name: Shared data layer
milestones.description: Define the TypeScript schema and ship the initial static data model used by the graph and panels.
deliverables: TypeScript types for Sinners, literary sources, themes, canto annotations, and entities
Static JSON data structure under src/data
Base Sinner dataset used by the graph

milestones.name: Lore graph MVP
milestones.description: Interactive D3 lore graph in React with detail panels and shared graph interactions.
deliverables: Cross-game entity node rendering and entity panel
Search entry point and graph selection flow

milestones.name: Lore graph full content
milestones.description: Expand the graph into a reliable reference tool with richer data, spoiler-aware canto context, source browsing, and consistency cleanup.
implemented_now: Canto annotations with spoiler gating in the UI
Filter panel for game, theme, and literary source
Shared literary group nodes in the graph
remaining_work: Data quality pass across Sinner literary sources and canto summaries
Consistency audit for graph links, shared groups, and panel interactions
Cross-game entity coverage review and cleanup
README and roadmap maintenance as the implementation changes

milestones.name: Sinner profile cards
milestones.description: Richer per-Sinner profile pages or expanded panels with cleaner presentation of lore, identities, EGOs, and timeline context.

milestones.name: Team builder (lore-aware)
milestones.description: Team composition tooling filtered by literary origin, theme, and affiliation rather than only combat mechanics.

milestones.name: Cross-game entity search polish
milestones.description: Improve search, deep links, and browse flows across Sinners, sources, and entity nodes.

next_action: Finish the M3 consistency pass: audit literary source links, shared-group behavior, canto coverage, and entity data quality before starting M4.

notes: README and roadmap were refreshed to match the implemented repo state.
M3 is partially implemented and now primarily a content-quality and consistency milestone.
